commands.command: iteration 3 addressed in commit `16891a2217` (now `44256f04f9` after cleanup): **[p2] narrativeâ†’bullets rotation drops or corrupts pending narrative text** you're right â€” `pushpreviewtoolprogress()` was calling `forcenewmessage()` without first awaiting any pending or in-flight narrative `chat.update`. because `draftstream.update()` is throttled and async, an in-flight narrative send could land *after* `forcenewmessage()` reset `lastsenttext`/`pendingtext`/`messageid`, leaving the agent's text segment either silently discarded (pending text dropped on `loop.resetpending()`) or written into what becomes the bullet preview slot â€” and then deleted at end-of-turn when `deletebulletpreview()` tears it down. fix: `pushpreviewtoolprogress()` is now `async`. before `forcenewmessage()` it awaits `draftstream.flush()`, which drains `pendingtext` and `inflightpromise` without stopping the loop (`clear()` is still off-limits â€” it calls `discardpending â†’ stop`). all six call sites are already in async callbacks (`ontoolstart`, `onitemevent`, `onplanupdate`, `onapprovalevent`, `oncommandoutput`, `onpatchsummary`), so they now `await pushpreviewtoolprogress(...)`. symmetric fix in `ondraftboundary`'s narrative branch: same race exists when an assistant-message boundary rotates a narrative draft. now also awaits `flush()` before `forcenewmessage()` so the pending agent text segment lands as a permanent message before id rotation. **rebase + housekeeping** - rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`00de585574`). resolved conflicts in `dispatch.ts`, `dispatch.streaming.test.ts`, and `docs/channels/slack.md` â€” all upstream simplifications kept; my behavior changes preserved on top. - dropped stale `chutes/models.ts` and `huggingface/models.ts` allowlist entries from `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` â€” upstream removed those raw fetches between rebase points. **test status** `pnpm test extensions/slack`: 89 files / 898 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean across all touched files. pushed (force-with-lease) to `44256f04f9`. ci re-running. the remaining ci failure on the previous run (`extension-runtime-dependencies.contract.test.ts > extensions/tlon does not keep unused direct runtime dependencies`) is pre-existing on `upstream/main` and unrelated to this pr.
commands.freeform_prompt: iteration 3 addressed in commit `16891a2217` (now `44256f04f9` after cleanup): **[P2] Narrativeâ†’bullets rotation drops or corrupts pending narrative text** You're right â€” `pushPreviewToolProgress()` was calling `forceNewMessage()` without first awaiting any pending or in-flight narrative `chat.update`. Because `draftStream.update()` is throttled and async, an in-flight narrative send could land *after* `forceNewMessage()` reset `lastSentText`/`pendingText`/`messageId`, leaving the agent's text segment either silently discarded (pending text dropped on `loop.resetPending()`) or written into what becomes the bullet preview slot â€” and then deleted at end-of-turn when `deleteBulletPreview()` tears it down. Fix: `pushPreviewToolProgress()` is now `async`. Before `forceNewMessage()` it awaits `draftStream.flush()`, which drains `pendingText` and `inFlightPromise` without stopping the loop (`clear()` is still off-limits â€” it calls `discardPending â†’ stop`). All six call sites are already in async callbacks (`onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary`), so they now `await pushPreviewToolProgress(...)`. Symmetric fix in `onDraftBoundary`'s narrative branch: same race exists when an assistant-message boundary rotates a narrative draft. Now also awaits `flush()` before `forceNewMessage()` so the pending agent text segment lands as a permanent message before id rotation. **Rebase + housekeeping** - Rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`00de585574`). Resolved conflicts in `dispatch.ts`, `dispatch.streaming.test.ts`, and `docs/channels/slack.md` â€” all upstream simplifications kept; my behavior changes preserved on top. - Dropped stale `chutes/models.ts` and `huggingface/models.ts` allowlist entries from `scripts/check-no-raw-channel-fetch.mjs` â€” upstream removed those raw fetches between rebase points. **Test status** `pnpm test extensions/slack`: 89 files / 898 tests pass. `pnpm run lint:tmp:no-raw-channel-fetch` passes. `oxfmt --check` clean across all touched files. Pushed (force-with-lease) to `44256f04f9`. CI re-running. The remaining CI failure on the previous run (`extension-runtime-dependencies.contract.test.ts > extensions/tlon does not keep unused direct runtime dependencies`) is pre-existing on `upstream/main` and unrelated to this PR.
